channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Calls for Moroccan rapper’s release outside a Casablanca court
item.description: Protesters gathered outside a court in Casablanca, Morocco to demand the release of Moroccan rapper Mehdi El Youbi.

item.title: Armed attackers kill more than 20 in northwest Nigeria
item.description: The attacks reflect a surge in violence as armed gangs continue raiding villages in northwest and central Nigeria.

item.title: In Gaza, a long path to recovery for one woman who lost eye, and daughter
item.description: Psychological wounds compound Najwa's physical injuries after losing her daughter in an Israeli air strike.

item.title: Why Abderrahim Fakir’s death reignited Italy’s police accountability debate
item.description: A Moroccan-born man’s death in Bologna has prompted anger and renewed scrutiny of Italy’s policing and legal reforms.

item.title: Spain and France battle raging wildfires, face high risk of more
item.description: A wildfire in Spain's Guadalajara region has burned 32,000 hectares, in one of the country's largest recorded blazes.

item.title: US tariffs ‘strengthen’ Lula against Bolsonaro’s son in upcoming elections
item.description: With Lula polling ahead and tariffs squeezing Brazil's economy, the Bolsonaro family's Washington strategy is faltering.

item.title: US House passes $1.15 trillion military bill expanding Israel cooperation
item.description: Bill to fund war on Iran and a handful of Trump priorities passes largely along party lines, faces Senate uncertainty.

item.title: AI, China, ‘dark factories’ and the future of the US auto industry
item.description: Economist Mary Lovely dismisses the claims that getting automakers to produce more cars in America proves tariffs work.

item.title: Iran war live: US kills 2, wounds 11; Houthis attack 2 Saudi oil tankers
item.description: US and Iran trade attacks as more explosions are reported in Iranian cities, including Sirik, Ahvaz and Bushehr.

item.title: Austrian home where Hitler was born reopened as police station
item.description: For decades, neo-Nazis and far-right extremists flocked to the Austrian home where Adolf Hitler.

item.title: Yemen’s Houthis claim attack on two Saudi oil tankers
item.description: Claimed attacks come days after Houthis announced naval embargo on Saudi vessels

item.title: Rubio says US, China must ‘work through’ big differences at ASEAN meeting
item.description: Rubio met his Chinese counterpart to discuss managing tensions ahead of President Xi Jinping's upcoming US visit.

item.title: Iranian missiles show deadly precision amid US-Iran war escalation
item.description: Killing of three US soldiers at Jordan base shows Iran maintains the ability to exact deadly toll in grinding conflict.

item.title: US launches new strikes as Iran warns of regional turmoil
item.description: Tehran's chief negotiator says if Iran's 'security is not ensured, no infrastructure will be safe'.

item.title: Wounded Gaza journalists call for urgent medical evacuation
item.description: Wounded and chronically ill Palestinian journalists held a protest calling for urgent medical evacuation from Gaza.

item.title: US and Saudi Arabia announce nuclear cooperation deal
item.description: Trump administration says 'historic' agreement will 'reinforce global nonproliferation standards' and create jobs in US.

item.title: India’s Rahul Gandhi speaks out after release
item.description: Indian opposition leader Rahul Gandhi has spoken out publicly for the first time since he was arrested during protests.

item.title: US regulators to weigh controversial peptides backed by RFK Jr
item.description: FDA has questioned the evidence as demand surges for the compounds promoted for sleep, muscle growth, and anti-ageing.

item.title: Ukraine’s new military chief pledges to escalate retaliation against Russia
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov is due to meet US counterpart Marco Rubio, but progress towards peace unlikely.

item.title: Griffiths: Diplomacy requires US and Iran to accept they have ‘lost‘
item.description: Griffiths: Diplomacy requires US and Iran to accept they have ‘lost‘

item.title: UN rights chief condemns Nicaragua’s decision to abolish elections
item.description: UN rights chief condemns worsening repression in Nicaragua, urging the restoration of freedoms and the rule of law.

item.title: Trump: Iran will ‘pay a big price’ for US troop deaths
item.description: US President Trump warned that Iran will ‘pay a big price’ right before he attended a dignified transfer ceremony.

item.title: Gaza man returns home after family believed he was dead
item.description: Hamada Al Banna reunited with his family more than a year after an explosion that left him in a coma.

item.title: Water cannon deployed at India education system protests
item.description: Indian police deployed water cannons in Raipur at student protests demanding the resignation of the education minister.

item.title: Key takeaways from Khalil al-Hayya’s first speech as Hamas leader
item.description: The newly appointed leader says Hamas would work to help the people of Gaza and achieve a united Palestinian state.
